title: [Ava Alert] Health Issue: high_memory_usage
description:
High memory usage: 95% of heap used

Suggested Action: Review health dashboard and remediate if needed
close_reason: False positive — fixed in PR #286 (v8 heap limit)

title: [Ava Alert] Board attention needed
description:
**ALERT: critical Empty Board State**

The Automaker development board is reporting **zero features** across all statuses. This is highly abnormal given the MEMORY.md shows the board should have 137 features (3 backlog, 126 done).

**Why this needs attention:**
1. This could indicate a data integrity issue with the feature storage system
2. The MCP connection to the Automaker server may be failing
3. The board query might be erroring silently
4. Given the Feb 10 feature data loss incident documented in MEMORY.md, this pattern is familiar

**Recommended immediate actions:**
1. Check if the dev server is running and healthy
2. Verify `.automaker/features/` directory contains feature.json files
3. Check server logs for errors in feature loading
4. Test MCP tool connectivity with a simple board query
5. If data is missing, restore from git history (feature.json files are now git-tracked per PR #164)

This requires immediate investigation before any other work proceeds.**ALERT: critical Empty Board State**

The Automaker development board is reporting **zero features** across all statuses. This is highly abnormal given the MEMORY.md shows the board should have 137 features (3 backlog, 126 done).

**Why this needs attention:**
1. This could indicate a data integrity issue with the feature storage system
2. The MCP connection to the Automaker server may be failing
3. The board query might be erroring silently
4. Given the Feb 10 feature data loss incident documented in MEMORY.md, this pattern is familiar

**Recommended immediate actions:**
1. Check if the dev server is running and healthy
2. Verify `.automaker/features/` directory contains feature.json files
3. Check server logs for errors in feature loading
4. Test MCP tool connectivity with a simple board query
5. If data is missing, restore from git history (feature.json files are now git-tracked per PR #164)

This requires immediate investigation before any other work proceeds.
close_reason: Stale alert — server was temporarily down. Board confirmed healthy: 137 features (0 backlog, 1 review, 126 done). No data loss.

title: Fix: Linear MCP search_issues returns object in text field instead of string — violates MCP content schema
close_reason: PR #311 created - vendored linear-mcp-server with patches for text field MCP schema violations. Three bugs fixed: array spread in addMetricsToResponse, object returns in all error handlers.

title: Zero-downtime staging deploy: survive Docker restarts during auto-mode
description: Every PR merge to main triggers deploy-staging.yml which restarts Docker, killing all running agents. Need blue-green deploy or graceful shutdown so auto-mode survives deploys. Current workaround: concurrency 1 + manual WIP commits. See MEMORY.md for full analysis.
notes: Solutions to evaluate: (1) Blue-green deploy with healthcheck swap, (2) Rolling update with start-first order, (3) Agent state persistence + auto-resume on startup, (4) Deploy batching or deploy-only-when-idle check

title: MCP tool to view server logs on crash
description: When the dev server crashes, Ava has no way to see the logs. Build an MCP tool (get_server_logs) that can tail the server log file or read the last N lines. Should work even when the server is down (read from log file, not API). Also useful for diagnosing agent failures, 401s, and OOM crashes.
close_reason: Implemented get_server_logs MCP tool in PR #275. Adds file transport to createLogger, server.log at data/server.log with 10MB rotation, and MCP tool that reads directly from disk. Ava + Frank prompts updated with server health monitoring.

title: [Ava Alert] Board attention needed
description:
**ALERT: critical No Features Found**

The board is reporting 0 total features across all statuses. This is either a data loss incident or a critical system failure preventing feature loading.

**Immediate Actions Required:**
1. Check if `.automaker/features/` directory exists and contains feature.json files
2. Verify the Automaker server is running and healthy
3. Check server logs for feature loading errors
4. If features exist on disk but aren't loading, this indicates a database/cache corruption issue

**Context:** This matches the pattern from the 2026-02-10 data loss incident where 141 features were recursively deleted. However, given recent sessions show 137 features (with 127 done), this is more likely a loading/cache failure than actual data loss.

---

**ALERT: high No In-Progress Work**

With 0 features in progress, no active development is occurring. Combined with the zero-feature count, this suggests either:
- Auto-mode is not running
- The board state is not loading correctly
- All work has genuinely completed (unlikely given session 33 mentions PRs #314 and #316 in CI)

**Action:** Verify auto-mode status and check if recent PRs (#314, #316) are tracked as features on the board.**ALERT: critical No Features Found**

The board is reporting 0 total features across all statuses. This is either a data loss incident or a critical system failure preventing feature loading.

**Context:** This matches the pattern from the 2026-02-10 data loss incident where 141 features were recursively deleted. However, given recent sessions show 137 features (with 127 done), this is more likely a loading/cache failure than actual data loss.

---

**ALERT: high No In-Progress Work**

With 0 features in progress, no active development is occurring. Combined with the zero-feature count, this suggests either:
- Auto-mode is not running
- The board state is not loading correctly
- All work has genuinely completed (unlikely given session 33 mentions PRs #314 and #316 in CI)

**Action:** Verify auto-mode status and check if recent PRs (#314, #316) are tracked as features on the board.

title: Design Flow Builder Agent — specialized crew member for scaffolding LangGraph workflows using test-driven flow development (prompt→tool→API→flow→production). Document the 5-layer pipeline pattern in ProtoLabs gold standard.
notes: Josh's thesis: Best systems start with a prompt defining data I/O → tool that calls inference + parses to JSON → API endpoint for testing → nodes compose into StateGraph with mock data → swap real models and fine-tune per-step. Each layer independently testable. FakeChatModel enables this pattern. Captures in ProtoLabs gold standard. Uses @automaker/flows, @automaker/llm-providers, @automaker/observability packages built in LangGraph Integration Foundation project.
